سارے جہاں سے اچھا ہندوستاں ہمارا
ہم بلبلیں ہیں اس کی، یہ گلستاں ہمارا
sāre jahā̃ se acchā, hindostā̃ hamārā.
ham bulbulẽ ha͠i iskī, yah gulistā̃ hamārā.

Better than the whole world, is our Hindustan;
We are its nightingales, and it is our flower-garden.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گُلِسْتَان (gulistān). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi گلستان (glstan /⁠gulistān⁠/). By surface analysis, گُل (gul, “flower”) + ـِسْتَان (-istān).

def: s.f. A knot; conglomeration; twist, entanglement; a tangled web; perplexity; — the bull's-eye (of a target)
subs: (meṅ), To fall into knots, &c.; to become knotted or tangled
(-kī), To take out the knots (of); to open a knot; to disentangle; to unravel
s.f. Mental uneasiness; soreness of heart; grief; displeasure; misunderstanding.

def: s.f. A knot; conglomeration; twist, entanglement; a tangled web; perplexity; — the bull's-eye (of a target)
subs: (meṅ), To fall into knots, &c.; to become knotted or tangled
(-kī), To take out the knots (of); to open a knot; to disentangle; to unravel
s.f. Mental uneasiness; soreness of heart; grief; displeasure; misunderstanding.

def: s.m. = P گلدم gul-dum, s.f. See s.v. gul.

ety: for orig. گله gula = S. गुड+dim. aff. ūla = S. ल or र
def: s.m. A small ball; a pellet; a marble.
s.m. A small ball; a pellet (such as is shot from a pellet-bow); a marble.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: s.f. A vessel used as a till or save-all; a small (hidden) niche in a wall to keep money in (cf. g̠alla).

s.f. A span (measure of circumference, i.e. the circle formed by joining the ends of the thumb and fore-finger); — a cob (of Indian corn, &c.); the flower of the keʼoṛā, q.v.; — a honey-comb; — the 'cat,' or short stick in the game of tip-cat (which is struck by the longer one called ḍanḍā); — gullī, s.f. A species of mainā (bird; — in this sense prob. fr. S. guṭikā); — a kind of pigeon; — a whetstone, a polishing stone
subs: or gillī-ḍanḍā, s.m. The game of tip-cat
To play at tip-cat; — to idle away (one's) time
lit. 'To make gillīs'; to argue or debate uselessly.

def: s.m. A cluster or clump of trees, a thicket; — a bush, a shrub; — a division of an army (consisting of nine platoons, each amounting to nine elephants, nine chariots, twenty-seven horse, and forty-five foot); — the spleen; a chronic enlargement of the spleen, or (as variously situated) any glandular enlargement in the abdomen (as that of the mesenteric gland, &c.), so as to be perceived externally.
s.m. See s.v. gul.
s.m. The ankle; — (f. -ā) A person with high ankles.
s.m. Name of a herb which grows in fields sown with ḵẖarīf grains.

ety: S. गोल, or गुल+ṛī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A small round cake of cow-dung.
s.m. See s.v. gul.
ex: the highest heaven is the warrior's garden.

ety: inf. n. of غلو 'to exceed the just or due limit (of
def: s.f. Excess; transgression, rebellion; commotion, tumult; noise, din, outcry, &c. ( = g̠ul, q.v.).

ety: or galū; Zend garaṅh, fr. gar; S. गल
def: s.m. The neck, throat, gullet, windpipe; — the voice
subs: (by Europeans, and native servants, galā-band), s.m. A collar; a scarf for the neck, a neckerchief, neck-tie; a cravat; — a small rectangular plate of gold (studded with gems) worn on the throat

adj. & s.m. Seizing by the throat; seizing or affecting the throat; — any acrid food (or drink) that constringes the palate or jaws; — being so affected in the throat (by trepidation or other cause) as to be unable to speak; one who seizes the throat (of); an accuser.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گلو (gulō). Cognate with Sanskrit गल (gala, “throat, neck”). Doublet of گلا (galā).

def: s.m. The pod of the mahuʼā tree, Bassia latifolia.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گلو (gulō). Cognate with Sanskrit गल (gala, “throat, neck”). Doublet of گلا (galā).

def: s.m. A monkey's pouch
s.m. = gilau, and guṛać, q.v.
s.m. A corr. form of گلزار gul-zār, q.v.s.v. gul.

ety: old P. gam; fr. Zend gam; S. गम्
def: adj. Lost; wanting; missing; absent; invisible; — wandering, distracted, confounded
subs: adj. & s.m. Losing the way; erring, wandering, astray; deviating; misled, seduced; abandoned, depraved; — one who has lost his way, one who is astray, &c.
v.t. To lose; to lead astray, to mislead, to seduce
s.f. A missing or losing (of) one's way; erring; error; deviation; seduction; — depravity; — apostasy
s.f. The state of being lost or astray; a lost condition
part. Lost; — s.m. One who (or that which) is lost
v.t. To lose; to hide; — v.n. To be proud
adj. Whose name is lost; wanting name, nameless; anonymous; extinct; — unknown to fame; ignoble, inglorious
adv. With the senses lost; senseless; stupefied
To be or become lost or missing, &c.; — gum ho-gayā, part. Lost ( = gum-shuda).

def: s.m. The plant Pharnaceum mollugo, and its fruit (it is used as a topic, and cure for ear-ache, ague, &c.).

ety: Pehl. gumān; Zend vī-manaṅh; S. वि+मनस्
def: s.m. Doubt, distrust, suspicion; surmise, conjecture; (in comp.) thinking; suspecting (e.g. bad-gumān, q.v.s.v. bad); — opinion, fancy, notion, supposition, imagination; — presumption; probability; — conceit, pride, haughtiness
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Full of conceit, puffed-up, proud
or gumān lānā, To think, imagine, suppose, believe, fancy, conjecture; — to doubt; to suspect, to mistrust; — to show pride, be conceited or proud
(-par), To run away with the idea (of); to have suspicion (against)
The supposition, &c. is; it is supposed, &c.; it is expected; it is probable or likely.

def: v.t. To lose; to let (a thing) be lost ( = gaṅwānā).
wety: From گم (gum, “lost, missing”) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: adj. & s.m. Suspicious, doubting, distrustful; — jealous; — conceited, vain, proud; — a suspicious character; — a conceited or proud person.

ety: perf. part. of gumāshtan, rt. gu-mār, fr. Zend vi+rt. mar (for hmar); S. वि+स्मर्
def: part. & s.m. Deputed, commissioned; appointed; set over; — a superintendent; commissary; deputy; representative; agent, factor; correspondent
subs: (-ko), To appoint (one) an agent, or manager, &c.
s.f. Commission; agency; office or business of an agent, &c.

def: s.f. Commission, delegation; charge; appointment; lieutenancy.
s.m. An arch; a vault; a dome, cupola; a tower; a bastion
subs: adj. Arched; vaulted; having a dome; dome-shaped; — convex
or gumbaẕ-kī āwāz, s.f. 'Echo of a dome'; the rebound or recoil of one's evil words or deeds on himself; requital, return (syn. badlā).

def: s.m. An arch; a vault; a dome, cupola; a tower; a bastion
subs: adj. Arched; vaulted; having a dome; dome-shaped; — convex
or gumbaẕ-kī āwāz, s.f. 'Echo of a dome'; the rebound or recoil of one's evil words or deeds on himself; requital, return (syn. badlā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گنبذ (gunbaz, gumbaz, “dome, arch, cupola”).

def: adj. Arched; vaulted; dome-shaped ( = gumbaẕ-dār).
s.f. (dialec.) = ghuṅgćī, q.v.
s.m. The separation of corn from the husk.
s.m. = gumbad = gummaṭ, q.v.

ety: prob. corr. of P. gumbad; cf. gummaj, and also gumṭā
def: s.m. A dome, &c. (see gumbad); — a tower; a bastion.
s.m. Swelling, lump, bump; bruise; protuberance; tumour; intumescence ( = gumṭā).
s.m. = gumṛā, and gumṭā, qq.v.
s.f. corr. of ghumrī, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Musty, mouldy; decayed; rotten.
s.m. = gumṛā or gūmṛā, qq.v
s.f. = gummaṭ, q.v.

ety: Pehl. gūn; Zend gaona; S. गुण
def: s.m. Colour; — kind, species; form, mode, fashion (used in comp., e.g. gul-gūṅ, adj. 'Rose-coloured, red')
subs: adj. Of different colours; of different sorts or kinds; variegated; various; diversified; motley.

ety: Pehl. gūn; Zend gaona; S. गुण
def: s.m. Colour; kind, sort, species; form, figure, fashion; mode, manner
subs: or yak-gūna, adj. Single, simple; — peculiar; — a little (syn. thorā-sā)

def: s.m. A string or thread; a rope; a track-rope; string (of a musical instrument); bow-string; (in Geom.) chord (of an arc); — (suffixed to a numeral) fold, times (e.g. do-guṇ, 'two-fold, double'; das-guṇ, 'ten-fold') — (in Arith.) multiplier; multiplication ( = guṇā); product (in multiplication); — a secondary element, a subordinate or unessential part (of an action, &c.); — a quality, peculiarity, property, attribute; power, faculty; a good quality, excellence, merit, virtue; good character; goodness, kindness, favour, benefit; skill, cleverness; sense, understanding; — cause, reason; — mode, method, manner; species; — benefit, advantage, good, gain; — excess; — remission or abatement (of a disease, &c.); — a property of humanity, an ingredient or constituent of nature (three of which are particularized, viz. Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas, or 'goodness, passion, and darkness,' or 'virtue, foulness, and ignorance'); (hence, in Hindū poetry) a term for the number three; — (in Gram.) a secondary or subordinate gradation of a vowel (opp. to vr̤iddhi)
subs: or gun-ābād, s.m. corr. of guṇāvād, q.v.

vulg. gun-ākar (˚ṇa+āk˚), s.m. A mine or multitude of merits; one endowed with all virtues; — a name of Buddha Śākya-muni, the founder of the Buddhist religion; — an epithet of Śiva; — adj. (f. -ā), Possessing all excellences; endowed with all virtues
vulg. guṇ-āgār (˚ṇa+āg˚), s.m. The abode or receptacle of good qualities or virtues; one endowed with all virtues; a person of surpassing excellence (syn. guṇākar, and guṇ-nidhān)
(˚ṇa+ag˚), s.m. Merit and demerit; virtues and vices; excellences and blemishes (of; — syn. guṇ-dosh)

(ṇa+an˚), s.m., and guṇāvād (˚ṇa+āv˚), s.m. Reciting the perfections and excellences (of), extolling, glorifying; eulogizing, eulogy, lauding, laudation; — gratefulness, thankfulness, thanks; — guṇānuvād karnā, or gānā (-kā), To hymn the praise (of), to extol, to glorify; to eulogize, laud, &c.
s.m. (f. -inī), One who recites the perfections, &c. (of another), extoller, praiser, &c.
vulg. gun-āyan (-ṇa+ay˚), s.m. (f. -ā or -kā), One who goes in the path of virtue, a virtuous person
s.m. The loss of good qualities or merits
or gun-phal, s.m. (in Arith.) A multiple
vulg. gunagya, s.m. (f. -ā), One who knows how to judge (of men and things), recognizer of merit, appreciator of the worth (of); a friend (of virtue)
(-kā), To pass over the good qualities (of)
(f. -ā), and guṇ-dāyak (f. -ikā), adj. Imparting excellence, or virtue, or benefit, &c.; beneficial, profitable; improving; efficient
s.m. Good qualities (or properties) and bad; merits and demerits; virtues and vices
s.m. The virtue (or duty) incident to the possession of certain qualities (e.g. clemency is the virtue and duty of royalty, &c.)
profit; — to be effective, to have effect ( = guṇ karnā); — to multiply (a number, into, -meṅ)
adj. & s.m. Endowed with all good qualities; — an ocean of good qualities; one endowed with all virtues; an epithet of the Supreme Being
s.f. Praising, lauding; eulogy, encomium, panegyric
(-ko), To return a favour, repay a benefit
(f. -ikā), or guṇ-kārī (f. -iṇī), adj. Productive of good qualities, profitable, beneficial, salutary, helpful; fit; effective, effectual; — guṇ-kārī, adj. & s.m. = guṇ-vān, or guṇī, qq.v.
(-ko), To be profitable or beneficial (to), to benefit, to improve, &c. ( = guṇ denā, q.v.)
s.m. A mine of excellence or merit; a very excellent, or skilful, or learned person (syn. guṇākar, gunāgār)
vulg. gun-gāth, s.m., or guṇ-gāthā, vulg. gun-gāthā, s.f. Statement of the qualities (of a person or thing, whether in praise or dispraise)

s.m. Singing the virtues or excellent qualities (of); praising, eulogizing; praise, laudation, eulogy, panegyric; — guṇ-gān karnā (-kā), To sing the praises (of); — to celebrate in song; to laud, eulogize, &c.; — to prattle or crow (as infants)

or guṇ-grāhak (f. -ikā), or guṇ-grāhī (f. -inī), adj. & s.m. Appreciating or discerning merit or qualities; doing justice to the good qualities (of); patronizing learning; — one who appreciates merit or good qualities; — a seeker after knowledge
(-kā), To acknowledge a favour or benefit; to be grateful (for)
adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of merits or virtues; abounding in excellences referable to the three fundamental qualities
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Destroying qualities, or virtues, &c.; — a destroyer of qualities; neutralizer; antidote
s.m. 'Treasury or depository of good qualities'; a person of great excellence or merit (syn. guṇākar, and guṇāgār, qq.v.)
vulg. gun-bāćak, s.m. (in Gram.) Any word indicating a quality or attribute; an adjective
s.m. Pointing out (or celebrating) good qualities or merits
or gun-wālā, adj. & s.m. (f. -wālī) = guṇ-vān, q.v.

or guṇ-want (f. -vatī), or gun-wantā (f. -ī), adj. & s.m. Endowed with good qualities, or virtues, or merits, or excellences; excellent, perfect; — meritorious; virtuous; worthy; accomplished, skilful, able; — an excellent person; a perfect man; a saint; an accomplished scholar; a skilful or clever person (syn. guṇī)
s.m. (in Gram.) An attributive; an adjective, an epithet
adj. (f. -ā), Void of properties or attributes; — void of merit, void of good qualities or endowments
(˚na+īś˚), s.m. lit. 'Lord of the three (fundamental) qualities'; name of a mountain (supposed by some to be Ćitrakoṭ in Bundelkhaṇḍ).
ex: اُس کے کوئی اَچّھے گُن ہَیں ہی نَہِیں۔
us ke koī acche gun ha͠i hī nahī̃.
He has no good qualities at all.

def: s.m. (f. gunī) Fold, times (suffixed to numerals to form multiplicatives; e.g. sau-gunā, 'a hundred-fold'); — multiplication; the rule of multiplication
subs: s.m. Multiplication; — the product of multiplication
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi ਗੰਨਾ (gannā) / گَنّا (gannā). Doublet of گَان٘ڈَا (gā̃ḍā).

def: adj. See s.v. gūn.

ety: Pārsī vanāh (cf. Pehl. venaś); rt. Zend nāś with vi; S. वि+नश्
def: s.m. Fault, offence, crime, sin; guilt; vice, iniquity (often contrac. to gunah, esp. in comp.)
subs: (-par), To impute crime (to); to accuse falsely
To commit a fault, or sin; to offend; to sin

(or gunah-gār; in Hindī gunah-kār, and gunah-kārī), s.m. A sinner; an offender, a culprit, a criminal; — gunāh-gār ṭhairanā (-ko), To make (one) out (to be) an offender, or a sinner, &c.; — to convict one of crime or offence, &c.; to convict; to condemn; to sentence

(or gunah-gārī; — in Hindī gunah-kārī), s.f. A state of sin; a habit of vice; — sinfulness; criminality; guiltiness; — a fine for an offence; mulct, penalty; — revenue derived from judicial fines; — loss, deficit
To pay a fine or penalty
adj. Without offence, innocent, guiltless; sinless.

def: s.m. contrac. of gunāh, q.v.: — gunah-gār: for this and other compounds see s.v. gunāh.
s.m. (in Arith.) The multiplier
s.m. See s.v. guṇ.

r: gunākar, guṇā-kār, guṇā, gunāh-gār, gunah-gār, gunāh
def: s.m. corr. 1˚ of guṇā-kār, q.v.s.v. guṇā; — 2˚ of gunāh-gār, or gunah-gār, q.v.s.v. gunāh.
s.m. Multiplication, the operation of multiplication; — counting, enumeration
s.m. The condition of a rope or string.
s.m. The condition of a rope or string.

ety: fr. guṇa, after the model of causals; see ginānā
def: v.t. To cause (one) to recite the good qualities of; to cause to praise or laud, &c.
s.f. The seed of Abrus precatorius (syn. ghūṅgćī).
s.f. A kind of fish, (one of the Siluridœ).
s.m. = guććhā, q.v.
corr. of gunj or kūṅć; and of kūć; and kūćā, qq.v.
s.m. A bud; a rose-bud; a blossom
subs: To be collected together (like a rose-bud); to be closely-packed, be densely populated, be crowded
adj. Having a mouth like a rose-bud; — s.m. A mouth like a rose-bud; — s.f. A mistress.
